page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m8.3xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by a 2.5 GHz Intel Xeon processor. It features 12 dedicated physical cores and 12 vCPUs running on a KVM hypervisor. The instance is configured with 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark results show a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the bottom 25% tier. However, multi-core operations, including stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores, reach average levels. Memory bandwidth tests also demonstrate average performance, with read-only small block speeds at 586.04 GB/sec and large block write speeds at 45.95 GB/sec.

This instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high single-threaded performance or local storage. It performs adequately in database operations, showing average results in Passmark database tests and Redis SET operations, which reached 10.27 million operations per second. However, it is poorly suited for medium-sized large language model inference, where prompt processing for a 7B model falls into the bottom 10% tier. It is best utilized for caching layers, memory-intensive web serving, and moderate multi-threaded processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m8.3xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance featuring 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 96.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high 8.0 GB per core ratio. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, this instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate weak single-core capabilities, but average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance. It achieves average results in database operations and Redis SET benchmarks, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads like caching, database hosting, and moderate multi-threaded applications. However, it exhibits poor performance in medium-sized LLM inference tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m8.3xlarge is a memory-optimized KVM instance with 12 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering average multi-core and database performance.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio for database operations and multi-threaded workloads without local storage.

High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core
Average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for database operations, caching, and web serving
